questions.explanation: MLAs are elected by the people and then become members of the legislative assembly.

questions.text: Who is the head of the state government who appoints the Chief Minister and other ministers?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a state is appointed by the Central Government to ensure the state works within the Constitution.

questions.text: The political party that has the majority of MLAs is called the:
questions.explanation: The party that has more than half the number of MLAs in a state is considered to be in a majority.

questions.text: A place where all the MLAs, from the ruling party as well as the opposition, meet to discuss various things is the:
questions.explanation: The Legislative Assembly is the venue for debates and decision-making for the state.

questions.text: The executive head of the state government is the:
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister and the cabinet of ministers are responsible for running various government departments.

questions.text: Who has the dual role of being an MLA and also a Minister?
options: Ministers selected by the Chief Minister
questions.explanation: Certain MLAs are assigned ministerial portfolios, giving them responsibilities in both the legislature and executive.

questions.text: What is the purpose of a press conference held by a minister?
options: To explain government steps and answer questions
questions.explanation: Press conferences are a way for the government to communicate its actions to the public through the media.

questions.text: The government can also decide to make new laws for the state regarding sanitation and health. This is done in the:
questions.explanation: Laws for the entire state are debated and passed in the Legislative Assembly.

questions.text: In a democracy, who has the main authority and elects the representatives?
questions.explanation: The people elect their representatives (MLAs) and thus have the main authority in a democracy.

questions.text: When no single party gets a majority and two or more parties come together, it is a:
questions.explanation: A coalition is formed when multiple parties combine their MLA counts to cross the majority mark.
